just wanted to put a few stats since people are constantly talking about reservation on economic basis unreserved category is close to 30 percent of population and have around 50 percent of seats open to them in institutions with reservations using data from plfs 2017 18 average urban male belonging to unreserved category with 18 years of formal schooling earns more than 20 000 and person belonging to sc category with same years of formal education less than 15000 and a person belonging to st category with similar education earns close to 13000 36 8 sc 26 7 st and 16 1 of others in rural area are poor it s 39 9 31 4 and 16 in case of urban areas source 71 of sc farmers are landless agriculture labourers under 5 mortality rate 56 per 1000 for people belonging sc category 57 for those from st category and 39 for unreserved category source the list can go on and on having said that i would like to reiterate that reservations were not meant for economic upliftment being a dalit has high social costs and passive discrimination has not ended if active discrimination has declined in some places which still occurs also a lot of people are also saying that benefit of reservation goes to a small minority of dalits while i would agree there s a problem in that regard how is doing away with reservations a solution to that if anything it makes a case for rationalization of the system poverty is a huge problem in india and there should be affirmative action for the poor of non dalit category also but it doesn t change the fact that in a similarly placed situation a non dalit is still having to bear lower social cost than a dalit if at all it calls for more affirmative action and not less in the end anticipating the obvious responses i would just say privilege is invisible to those who have it let s all reflect on and question those we possess and those we don t edit changed general to unreserved

good post thanks for sharing your experience few points i m a proud hindu this is not a good thing since you used the semantic proud which has very specific meaning this sounds like stockholm syndrome like dynamic if a system keeps you down and you are proud of that system something dramatically wrong has gone in analysis a better framing may be like i am culturally hindu or hindu adjacent or part of hinduism multiple traditions in cultural sense like ritual greeting naming conventions food marriage customs new home prayer customs etc being proud says something entirely different you see this caste thing is not some quantifiable metric it s this abstract and subjective emotion if is not abstract and is indeed quantifiable you yourself echo this here in part i am privileged in the sense that my father enabled me to have a decent financial upbringing despite the discrimination he faced i am oppressed still simply because i am not you this oppression and discrimination is very discrete and clear it s caste based not class based caste system is a specialized form of class system the latter existed and exists in all scaled human societies that is not new caste system has no equal in human history it s unique it has produced endogamic communities living literally meters apart for 1000s of years with 0 genetic cross over this is not normal anywhere in the world and reservation is a net positive but short term tool with negative long term curve meaning longer current reservation system is kept in place in it s current form the worse things will get for society at large the only true and long term solution is what every other human societies did and why they didn t end up forming a caste system in practical terms even if they weren t aware of it and that being inter mixing of caste blood is thicker than water even more so when its backbone is longer than the very existence of state or even many sects of hinduisms this is why caste dynamic has crossover even into indian christian and muslim and even sikh religions caste in india exists on a higher hierarchy than even religions doctrine because blood is indeed thicker than water or any other meme the state has to introduce over the top incentives for inter caste unions and their children this works because self interest has a higher hierarchy than caste or blood because self interest is blood interest and when majority are inter caste caste no longer can be used as the tool overtly or informally indirectly it will cross the tipping point phenomenon of how a society at large conducts itself with each other this is the only solutions since even if india achieves developed status but retains blood based caste systems in 80 100 years that is very bad on a list of things too long to list in this comment

life s like this statistically if you work hard you will have a much better chance at getting out of poverty stay lazy do bare minimum wish for betterment without putting work into it and you ll most like stay poor i was born into a lower middle class farmer family i remember when i d walk to school and back with this heavy ass bag on my shoulders the village people and their kids about my age sitting under the tree shades drinking tea and playing cards would look at me and say stuff like why u getting fried in this hot sun u a labourer why is the bag so heavy whats in the bag bricks what will u ever gain by blackening papers all day then when in evenings after the games cricket volleyball etc the rest of the kids would keep roaming around till late and i d leave for home they d be like yo why u leaving so early its barely sunset school starts at midnight or what all u gotta do is sleep you have whole night for that don t you anyway half of those kids are now dead from drug overdoses and liver failures or murdered overseas so are their elders the remaining half are right where i last saw them some sold their land to make ends meet some sold them to go abroad by dunky because they couldn t clear ielts toefl or whatever to go by regular means and they were left on the mercy of whatever came across their journey who would either let them pass or kill them where they stood looking at some of them anyone seeing them for the first time would feel pity and say they are underprivileged and must be helped but me looking back at them i realize they were born and brought up in similar households had same free time had same financial condition i didn t have any special privilege back then only difference was when they chose to have fun in their early years i kept on with my studies when they got into drinking in the name of friendship and fun i decided to stay away now another example here me and wife we re working our jobs we get up everyday and go to work no matter what even if i m not feeling like going or i m mildly sick or have something come up i always adjust and give priority to work because that s what brings money home wife has multiple health issues and she still pushes through we shifted to a new place recently and after two months we decided to hire a maid to make things easier the one we hired a middle aged lady would come very late in evening while we would just come home from work and stay there waiting for her to do the cleaning before we could do rest of the work we told her many times to come early so we can make use of our time but to no avail many a times she would skip the work without even informing us and then we d have to clean ourselves later in the night even then she would just do bare minimum the floors were never properly cleaned the utensils had unwashed detergent on them when we using them later for dinner and upon confronted she would just deny that her work is bad and we ended up firing her cause we hired her make things easier and it was the other way around when we did that she came to us like you are taking away a poor person s livelihood we stayed without maid for few months before hiring another and one we hired now she s a young teenager regarding work same story first week she was good and then she started doing the same thing took 5 leaves in first 20 days made excuses to leave early and left work unfinished few days ago my wife asked her if she goes to college school during day she said she left school after 8th cause she didn t want to study we told her to fill admission forms in a govt school and give exam at the least it costs next to nothing but even by barely passing the 10th she can be eligible for much more jobs like helper sweeper at govt offices etc and earn well she just shrugged it off which made me think some people are poor not because they are unlucky but because they just don t want to work study for their betterment so if you re feeling privileged its not always because you re lucky but you worked hard for it

have always thought of writing something similar but i didn t want to because i thought it would be fruitless reading your story i felt i should share my journey too as there are lot of similarities my mom and dad come from a remote village in gujarat we belong to obc he was one of the first few peoples from our community to come to mumbai and similar to you we have extended family still in village although now things have changed and a lot of people have moved to ahmedabad mumbai for jobs until i got a job we lived as lower middle class my father is only 10th pass and there was never discussion on caste till i myself reached 10th std once i reached there even then i didn t understand the whole caste system thing except the fact that i will get better seat if i did have a certificate i scored pretty well in 10th and yet that was not enough to get into one of colleges and courses i wanted i used my certificate once to get admission in junior college 11th 12th std however when it came to iit jee i wasn t so sure and felt pretty bad about taking the reservation previously you can say i got more exposure about the issue and understood what it meant in real world thankfully i had bunch of friends around me who didn t make a big deal about it and were pretty supportive either way while filling form i decided not to use my reservation yes i did not get what i wanted and after the results i did feel regret of not using reservation but still i got into pretty good institute and did well right now i am in pretty good government job and have not taken any advantage of my caste certificate since that admission in junior college it was only after getting my job that situation of my family improved and we are comfortable now as well as happy comparing life and childhood of my peers i think they have lived a much more comfortable life and there was definitely a generational economic disadvantage for us i still get weird reactions from people who after hearing my surname think that i have got here due to reservation govt job so i am tired of general category people discussing how reserved category people are looters and how it discourages merit when i try to explain that whole thing is not so black and white and it s not just about economic elevation they tell me i will never know how they feel because i too belong to other side person in concern had grandfather working in british government as a judge any i have got used to such comments and now just ignore them i don t have much to say as far as my career is concerned but hearing your story made me think and share about mine thank you for sharing it

adani group passed off low quality coal as far more expensive cleaner fuel in transactions with an indian state power utility according to evidence seen by the financial times that throws fresh light on allegations of a long running coal scam the documents secured by the organized crime and corruption reporting project occrp and reviewed by the ft add a potential environmental dimension to accusations of corruption associated with the indian conglomerate they suggest that adani may have fraudulently obtained bumper profits at the expense of air quality since using low grade coal for power means burning more of the fuel invoices show that in january 2014 adani purchased an indonesian shipment of coal said to contain 3 500 calories per kilogramme the same shipment was sold to the tamil nadu generation and distribution company tangedco as 6 000 calorie coal one of the most valuable grades adani appears to have more than doubled its money in the process after transport costs the ft has also matched documentation for a further 22 shipments in 2014 involving the same parties that indicates a pattern of grade inflation in the supply of 1 5mn tonnes of coal adani sourced the coal in indonesia from a mining group known for its low calorie output at prices consistent with low grade fuel it delivered the coal to india s southern most state for power generation fulfilling a contract that specified expensive high quality fuel more than 2mn people are killed in india each year by outdoor air pollution according to a 2022 study in the lancet while other studies found significant increases in child mortality for hundreds of miles around coal fired power plants

here s my attempt at an honest answer i m not a north indian i m from west india but i guess all of us are categorized as northies down south so i guess it doesn t matter all the states i ve been to for extended durations in the north like rajasthan west bengal haryana punjab maharashtra everywhere hindi just works you don t feel the urge to need the language so you aren t putting an active effort eventually you end up learning a few words on top of it most of these languages are very similar to hindi it s very easy to learn gujarati marathi bengali if you know hindi however things are very different in the south india since none of the languages are aryan languages and are fairly different from hindi it doesn t come naturally to learn these languages i came to karnataka about 1 5 years ago and in my kannada learning journey i was overwhelmed the grammar is totally different the tenses the structure of the sentences and of course the script if learning marathi is 3 10 difficulty learning kannada is 8 10 for a typical northie that being said i had to actively make an effort to learn instead of gradually learning it subconsciously and it worked now i can hold a conversation in kannada despite not being very fluent i can also read the script now except some joined alphabets which i struggle with i went to hyderabad and found that telugu script is very similar to kannada script i can kind of read telugu as well once you get a hold of one language the others become easy but one needs conscious consistent efforts to do it of course this by no means is an excuse to not learn a language if you re residing in a state you should definitely at least try to live like the locals when in rome do as the romans do
